title: Lakshmi Devi Temple
tagline: Lakshmi Devi Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Lakshmi Devi temple is an early 12th-century Hindu temple dedicated for Goddess Mahalakshmi, who is considered to be a jagrat (awake) devi in Doddagaddavalli village in Hassan
overview.description: The Lakshmi Devi temple is an early 12th-century Hindu temple dedicated for Goddess Mahalakshmi, who is considered to be a jagrat (awake) devi in Doddagaddavalli village in Hassan District, Karnataka, India. It is the first Hoysala Temple constructed by the erstwhile rulers of the dynasty. The main temple has four-shrines that share a common mandapa (hall), each sanctum being a square and aligned to a cardinal direction. The eastern shrine houses the Hindu goddess Mahalakshmi, the northern shrine is dedicated to Kali, the western to Shiva—who is worshipped as Lakshmi’s brother—and the southern

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lakshmi Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lakshmi Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lakshmi Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (119 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (111 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lakshmi Devi Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lakshmi Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lakshmi Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Lakshmi Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lakshmi Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adavibantenahalli, Bylahalli, Agalahalli, Adagooru.
